title: Habenaria intacta
intro: Habenaria intacta, also known as the "Himalayan Orchid," is a species of orchid native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its unique and delicate appearance. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing fever, improving respiratory health, and preventing the spread of infections. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria intacta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Habenaria intacta is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets, to harness its health benefits and treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Habenaria intacta is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This genus, Habenaria, is a group of terrestrial orchids, commonly known as orchid spiders or terrestrial orchids. The classification of Habenaria intacta includes its kingdom as Plantae, its phylum as Magnoliophyta, its class as Liliopsida, its order as Asparagales, and its family as Orchidaceae.
